Meta.title: OSSFIT
Meta.description: Find living open source repos that match your stack.

Home.tagline: Discover active GitHub repositories tailored to your skills.

Beta.title: Beta access
Beta.description: OSSFIT is in a closed beta. Enter the access password you were given.

Login.title: Sign in with GitHub
Login.description: Sign in with your GitHub account and OSSFIT will recommend repositories that match your stack.

Guide.title: Contribution guide
Guide.intro: New to open source? Follow these five steps end-to-end.
Guide.step1Description: Fork the repository you are interested in to your account using the Fork button in the top right on GitHub.
Guide.step2Description: Download your fork to your machine. Replace {user} with your GitHub username.
Guide.step3Description: Never commit directly to main. Create a dedicated branch for your change.
Guide.step4Description: Commit in small steps and write a one-line message that captures why you made the change.
Guide.step5Description: Click Compare & pull request on GitHub. Reference the issue number in the body so reviewers get the context.
Guide.tip1: Comment on the issue first so your work doesn't overlap with someone else's.
Guide.tip2: If the repo links a CONTRIBUTING.md, read it before opening a PR.
Guide.tip3: Keep PRs small and focused — one change per PR.
